MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD ALHAJMOHAMMAD'S DEMURRER AND, ALTERNATIVELY, MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT:

Cross-defendant MOHAMMAD ALHAJMOHAMMAD's demurrer to Nicolas Jimenez's cross complaint is:

Sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 3 for common carrier negligence. A common carrier owes a heightened duty of care to passengers, not the public at large. (See Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 785.) Here, paragraph 38 of the cross-complaint shows that plaintiff-not Jimenez-was harmed by cross-defendant's alleged negligence. Jimenez is not entitled to recover on this claim merely because he has been named as a party in this suit. This ruling does not bar Jimenez from raising movant's common carrier duty and advisement that it was clear to exit the vehicle when litigating the indemnity claim.

Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to cause of action 4 for unfair business practices. An unfair, unlawful and/or deceptive business practice must be pleaded with reasonable particularity. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 619.) Here, the cross-complaint alleges that movant was an employee/agent of Uber and Uber violated the UCL. (Cross-complaint, pars. 15, 40-50.) There are no allegations regarding how movant driver individually owed the adduced insurance duties or deceptively advertised.

Sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 8 for tort of another. The only reasonable reading of the cross-complaint is that Alhajmohammad and Jimenez were joint tortfeasors. Therefore, the tort of another doctrine does not apply. (See Vacco Industries, Inc. v. Van Den Berg (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 34, 56-57; Davis v. Air Technical Industries, Inc. (1978) 22 Cal.3d 1, 5.)

Sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 9 for declaratory relief and cause of action 10 for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Jimenez does not respond to this demurrer and apparently concedes that he does not have a contract with movant that warrants relief under these claims.

The motion to strike is off calendar as moot.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANTS RASIER, LLC. and UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

Off calendar for failing to comply with the meet and confer requirements of CCP Sec. 430.41. First, the code provides that the parties "shall meet and confer in person or by telephone." (CCP Sec. 430.41(a).) The Kline declaration fails to indicate compliance with the statute. Rather, it appears only that e-mails and written correspondences were exchanged. Second, cross-complainant's counsel declares that Uber's counsel has raised new issues in this demurrer that the parties never previously examined, which is contrary to CCP Sec. 430.41(a)(1) ["As part of the meet and confer process, the demurring party shall identify all of the specific causes of action that it believes are subject to demurrer"]. (See Ghantous Decl., par. 7.) The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately in compliance with the code and Uber's (RASIER, LLC, UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.) response to the cross-complaint is now due by October 14, 2021.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
